title: How do you find and trade good penny stocks?
author: irene-siller244
selftext:
I have always try to stay away from penny stocks especially as I have little knowledge on it. Recently, I decided to do some research to see if it might ignite my interest and realized that being early and strong smallcap community often positions one for good profit. This is why memecoin like $COMP, $INTR, $HL, $WTF etc have survived.

Example I've been really focused on the AI boom lately and how it's completely changing the economy. It's fascinating to watch these huge tech companies—the "big whales"—race to the top. They're not just competing for talent; they're also battling over chip production, money, and even new regulations. I was lucky enough to get into an AI-related stock before the trend officially took off and made a decent profit.

That experience taught me that spotting a big, lasting trend is a crucial part of my strategy for the next decade. That's why I've been keeping an eye on some smallcap like $WTF.

What are your thoughts on this? Do you agree that a long-term trend is the most important thing to look for?

comments.author: Recent_Worry4608
comments.body: Yeah, trend spotting’s key. I watch the fundamentals and the hype, but for smallcaps I like having solid real-time data…. bigshort.com been handy for that.

comments.author: chillguy89_vn
comments.body: You should have a consistent system to help you identify penny stocks that meet some of your criteria. You must have criteria to filter out garbarge
